p.seg: Anselm of Whitton sends greeting to all sons of Holy Mother Church both present as well as to come.

Let present as well as future know me to have given and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the monastic house of Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, in perpetual alms my whole peatery which is [between] Mowhope and Berhope and ‘Herdstrete’ which divides the land of Mow and Hownam, for the soul of King David, and Earl Henry, and King Malcolm, and for the salvation of King William, and David his brother, and for my own soul and my wife’s and my childrens’ souls. And so the monks of Melrose shall hold the said peatery in pure and everlasting alms, quit and free from all aids and all customs. And I and my heirs shall warrant to the said monks the same peatery in respect of all men for ever. In addition I have given to them in everlasting alms in my wood of Mowhope as much firewood as one horse can carry to their grange of Hownam each year from Easter to the Nativity of Saint Mary, by the same liberty by which I gave them the peatery.
